beats.narration: Spin a bolt-hole probe at three thousand RPM and the notch, the wobble, and the grass arrive on a strict schedule. Filters pull them apart.

payload.title: A NOTCH GOING IN CIRCLES
payload.sub: Filters separate signals by how fast the trace wiggles.

beats.narration: Filters never touch the two-hundred-kilohertz carrier. They work on motion frequency: wobble at fifty hertz, the millisecond notch near five hundred, grass everywhere.

payload.heading: Motion sets the signal frequency
payload.note: WOBBLE 50 Hz · NOTCH ~500 Hz · GRASS BROADBAND

beats.narration: So bracket the notch. Raise the high-pass until the wobble dies, lower the low-pass until the grass dies, leaving the notch at full height.
Push past that point and you over-filter. The notch shrinks, goes bipolar, rotates in phase, while the screen looks beautifully clean. Prove it.
Then drop to fifteen hundred RPM and the clean setup fails: signal frequency rides on speed. Re-optimize, verify on the notch, re-standardize.

payload.sub: Filters are valid only at the speed where you set them.
